summary:
The baby developed symptoms due to Toxoplasma gondii infection because despite being treated prenatally by Spiramycin which targets it effectively during certain stages, there might have been a delay causing progression into more severe forms requiring additional medications after birth. Agent1 concludes based largely off drug response patterns observed historically compared across multiple pathogens including CMVs etc., pointing specifically at latent stage persistence influencing outcome negatively when primary intervention faltered temporality-wise relative other options considered herein presented evidence base analysis.

Agent1 concludes based largely off drug response patterns observed historically compared across multiple pathogens including CMVs etc., pointing specifically at latent stage persistence influencing outcome negatively when primary intervention faltered temporality-wise relative other options considered herein presented evidence base analysis.
